@graph.name: PASSR
@graph.description: An engineering publication focused on practical writing for software teams building and operating production systems.

@graph.name: PASSR
@graph.description: Engineering writing on systems, APIs, and infrastructure.

@graph.name: PASSR
@graph.description: PASSR helps software engineers stay up to date on backend systems, infrastructure, databases, security, architecture, and the tools teams rely on in production. Articles are written for practitioners who ship code and run on-call rotations.

audience.audienceType: Software engineers, backend developers, infrastructure engineers, engineering managers

about.name: Backend engineering
about.description: API design, webhooks, job queues, service architecture

about.name: Infrastructure
about.description: Deployments, observability, blue-green releases, on-call practices

about.name: Software architecture
about.description: API versioning, RPC vs REST, system design tradeoffs

about.name: Developer tooling
about.description: TypeScript, CI/CD, product and platform reviews

@graph.contentPurpose: Publish practical, experience-driven engineering articles so readers can stay current on how teams build, deploy, secure, and operate modern software.
@graph.publishingPrinciples: No vendor pitches. Concrete examples over abstract advice. Written by practitioners for practitioners.
